threshold_count: Deficits in both intellectual and adaptive functioning
duration_rule: Onset during the developmental period

symptoms.description: The patient has deficits in intellectual functions, such as reasoning, problem solving, planning, abstract thinking, judgment, and learning from experience.
The patient has deficits in adaptive functioning that result in a failure to meet developmental and sociocultural standards for personal independence and social responsibility.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: IQ range of 71–84
duration_rule: Not specified; typically life-long traits

symptoms.description: The patient has an IQ in the range of 71 to 84, indicating borderline intellectual functioning.
The patient exhibits an absence of coping problems typically associated with intellectual developmental disorder.

threshold_count: 3/3 social communication deficits AND at least 2/4 restricted/repetitive behaviors
duration_rule: Symptoms present from early childhood

symptoms.description: The patient exhibits deficits in social-emotional reciprocity, ranging from abnormal social approach and failure of normal conversation to reduced sharing of interests, emotions, or affect.
The patient exhibits deficits in nonverbal communicative behaviors used for social interaction, ranging from poorly integrated communication to abnormalities in eye contact and body language.
The patient exhibits deficits in developing, maintaining, and understanding relationships, ranging from difficulties adjusting behavior to suit social contexts to difficulties in making friends.
The patient engages in stereotyped or repetitive motor movements, use of objects, or speech, such as simple motor stereotypies, echolalia, or idiosyncratic phrases.
The patient insists on sameness, inflexible adherence to routines, or ritualized patterns of verbal or nonverbal behavior, often showing extreme distress at small changes.
The patient exhibits highly restricted, fixated interests that are abnormal in intensity or focus, such as a strong attachment to unusual objects or excessively circumscribed interests.
The patient exhibits hyper- or hypo-reactivity to sensory input or an unusual interest in sensory aspects of the environment, such as indifference to pain or adverse responses to specific sounds.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Failure to meet expected milestones in several areas of functioning
duration_rule: Child is under the age of 5

symptoms.description: The patient fails to meet expected developmental milestones in several areas of intellectual functioning, and is under the age of 5.
The patient is unable to undergo reliable systematic assessments of intellectual functioning, typically due to being too young.

threshold_count: Cannot be reliably assessed due to physical or mental impairment
duration_rule: Child is at least 5 years old

symptoms.description: The patient has intellectual functioning that cannot be reliably assessed by standardized testing due to sensory, physical, or severe behavioral impairments.

threshold_count: Reduced vocabulary, limited sentence structure, and impaired discourse
duration_rule: Onset in the early developmental period

symptoms.description: The patient has a reduced vocabulary, demonstrating limited word knowledge and usage.
The patient demonstrates limited sentence structure, struggling to put words and word endings together to form sentences based on the rules of grammar and morphology.
The patient exhibits impaired discourse, struggling to connect sentences to explain or describe a topic or have a conversation.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Persistent difficulties in the social use of verbal and nonverbal communication
duration_rule: Onset in the early developmental period

symptoms.description: The patient exhibits deficits in the social use of verbal and nonverbal communication.
The patient has difficulty adapting their communication to fit the context or the needs of the listener.
The patient has difficulty following the rules for conversation and storytelling, such as taking turns or rephrasing when misunderstood.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Persistent difficulty with speech sound production that interferes with speech intelligibility
duration_rule: Onset in early childhood

symptoms.description: The patient experiences persistent difficulty with speech sound production.
The patient has limited speech intelligibility that significantly interferes with effective communication.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Frequent disruptions in the normal fluency and time patterning of speech
duration_rule: Onset in early childhood

symptoms.description: The patient experiences frequent disruptions in the normal fluency and time patterning of their speech.
The patient exhibits marked physical tension while speaking or produces words with excess tension.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: At least 1 symptom in reading, math, or written expression markedly below expected level
duration_rule: Persistent for at least 6 months despite intervention

symptoms.description: The patient has academic skills that are markedly below the expected level for their chronological age.
The patient scores significantly below norms on standardized tests in reading, mathematics, or writing.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Symptoms characteristic of communication disorder but lacking specific information
duration_rule: Not specified

symptoms.description: The patient exhibits symptoms characteristic of a communication disorder that cause impairment, but there is insufficient information to specify the exact diagnosis.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Motor skill performance is markedly below expectations for age and intelligence
duration_rule: Onset in the early developmental period

symptoms.description: The patient exhibits motor skill performance that is markedly below expectations for their chronological age and intelligence.
The patient experiences motor coordination difficulties that significantly interfere with academic progress or activities of daily living.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Repetitive, seemingly driven, and purposeless motor behavior
duration_rule: Onset in the early developmental period

symptoms.description: The patient engages in repetitive, abnormally frequent, non-goal-directed movements.
The patient exhibits motor behavior that significantly interferes with their social or academic activities.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Multiple motor AND 1 or more vocal tics
duration_rule: Longer than 1 year; onset before age 18

symptoms.description: The patient experiences rapid, recurrent, nonrhythmic motor movements, such as eye blinking or facial grimacing.
The patient exhibits abrupt, recurrent vocalizations, such as grunting, throat clearing, or uttering specific words.
The patient has a persistent history of experiencing multiple motor and vocal tics.

diagnosis_name: Persistent (Chronic) Motor or Vocal Tic Disorder
threshold_count: Single or multiple motor OR vocal tics, but NOT both
duration_rule: Longer than 1 year; onset before age 18

symptoms.description: The patient experiences single or multiple motor or vocal tics, but not both, persisting for longer than one year.
The patient has a persistent history of experiencing multiple motor and vocal tics.

threshold_count: Single or multiple motor and/or vocal tics
duration_rule: Less than 1 year; onset before age 18

symptoms.description: The patient experiences single or multiple motor and/or vocal tics that have been present for less than one year.
The patient has a persistent history of experiencing multiple motor and vocal tics.

threshold_count: Tics that do not meet full criteria for a specific tic disorder
duration_rule: Not specified

symptoms.description: The patient exhibits tics that cause distress or impairment but do not meet the full criteria for a specific tic disorder.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: 6 or more symptoms (5 for adults 17+) of inattention or hyperactivity
duration_rule: 6 months; onset before age 12

symptoms.description: The patient often fails to give close attention to details or makes careless mistakes in schoolwork, at work, or during other activities.
The patient often has difficulty sustaining attention in tasks or play activities.
The patient often does not seem to listen when spoken to directly.
The patient often does not follow through on instructions and fails to finish schoolwork, chores, or duties in the workplace.
The patient often has difficulty organizing tasks and activities.
The patient often avoids, dislikes, or is reluctant to engage in tasks that require sustained mental effort.
The patient often loses things necessary for tasks or activities.
The patient is often easily distracted by extraneous stimuli.
The patient is often forgetful in daily activities.
The patient often fidgets with or taps their hands or feet, or squirms in their seat.
The patient often leaves their seat in situations where remaining seated is expected.
The patient often runs about or climbs in situations where it is inappropriate, or experiences subjective feelings of restlessness if an adolescent or adult.
The patient is often unable to play or engage in leisure activities quietly.
The patient is often "on the go," acting as if they are driven by a motor.
The patient often talks excessively.
The patient often blurts out an answer before a question has been completed.
The patient often has difficulty waiting their turn.
The patient often interrupts or intrudes on others during conversations or activities.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Prominent symptoms of ADHD that do not fulfill full criteria
duration_rule: Symptoms present after age 12 or below threshold count

symptoms.description: The patient exhibits prominent ADHD symptoms that fall below the required diagnostic threshold count.
The patient first exhibited symptoms of ADHD after age 12.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.

threshold_count: Symptoms characteristic of a neurodevelopmental disorder but lacking full criteria
duration_rule: Onset before adulthood

symptoms.description: The patient exhibits symptoms characteristic of a neurodevelopmental disorder but lacks the full criteria for a specific diagnosis.
The patient experiences clinically important distress or impairment in social, occupational, or other important areas of functioning due to the symptoms.
